title: AI should help us produce better code
description: Willison argues that if adopting coding agents reduces code quality, developers should address that problem directly rather than accepting lower quality. Shipping worse code with agents is a choice - we can choose to ship better code instead. Part of his 'Agentic Engineering Patterns' series.
author: Simon Willison

title: Perhaps not Boring Technology after all
description: Simon Willison challenges the concern that LLMs will push technology choices only toward well-represented tools in training data. With latest models and long context windows, coding agents work excellently with brand new, private, or niche tools by consuming documentation and iterating. A surprising result - agents don't seem to enforce 'boring technology' choices.
author: Simon Willison

title: State of AI in 2026: LLMs, Coding, Scaling Laws, China, Agents, GPUs, AGI
description: Lex Fridman Podcast episode #490 with Sebastian Raschka and Nathan Lambert covers comprehensive state of AI in 2026 including LLMs, AI coding and programming tools, scaling laws, AI development in China, AI agents, GPU infrastructure, and AGI discussions. Topics include technical breakthroughs, open vs closed LLMs, and programming tooling evolution.
author: Lex Fridman, Sebastian Raschka, Nathan Lambert

title: Can coding agents relicense open source through 'clean room' implementation?
description: Explores how coding agents are extraordinarily good at building 'clean room' implementations of code, raising licensing questions. Also covers GPT-5.4 release with 1M token context window and improved benchmark performance, particularly on spreadsheet modeling tasks.
author: Simon Willison

title: Should you care about 'Good Code' in 2026?
description: Hacker News discussion explores how the definition of 'good code' (readable, well-structured, maintainable) is changing when AI writes implementations. The community debates what matters in an era of AI-generated code.
author: Hacker News Community

title: What AI coding costs you
description: Hacker News discussion mentioning Anthropic's CEO prediction that AI would write 90% of code within 3-6 months of March 2025, and explores what actually happened. Examines the real costs and trade-offs of adopting AI coding tools.
author: Hacker News Community

title: The unhinged world of tech in 2026
description: Fireship video discussing technology predictions for 2026, including AI helping with household chores versus humans helping fix AI code. Questions whether 2026 will represent a 'tech peak' or a 'crazy bubble' with coverage of AI coding and programming trends.
author: Fireship

title: AI Made Writing Code Easier. It Made Being an Engineer Harder
description: Hacker News discussion on how AI affects software engineering roles, exploring the shifting skill requirements and challenges engineers face as AI takes over more coding tasks.
author: Hacker News Community

title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (March 2026)
description: Monthly HN thread featuring community projects including Vox Labs, an AI-powered outbound sales platform for founders and agencies. Showcases practical AI applications developers are building in 2026.
author: Hacker News Community
